app.name: GitLab CI Dashboard
app.one_liner: A self-hosted dashboard providing global visibility into GitLab CI pipelines and schedules across entire groups, with server-side caching and optional pipeline control.

screens.name: Pipeline Overview
screens.purpose: Display all latest pipeline statuses, schedules, and job details within a GitLab group with filtering and search capabilities.

rust_commands.name: fetch_pipelines
rust_commands.purpose: Retrieve and cache pipeline data from GitLab API for a given group using server-side token authentication.

rust_commands.name: trigger_pipeline_action
rust_commands.purpose: Perform write operations on pipelines (restart, cancel, create) via GitLab API when configured with a read/write token.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add filtering and search functionality
week 1: Build main screen UI with pipeline and schedule tables
week 1: Implement server-side GitLab API client with caching
week 1: Support read-only token for monitoring
week 2: Add support for read/write token actions (restart/cancel/start)
week 2: Write acceptance tests for core workflows
acceptance_tests: Given a valid read_api token and GitLab group, the dashboard displays all latest pipeline statuses without rate limiting.
The dashboard allows filtering pipelines by status (e.g., failed) and by project name within the group.
When a user clicks 'Restart' on a failed pipeline and a read/write token is configured, the pipeline restarts successfully via GitLab API.
